# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

```groovy
import jenkins.model.*
import hudson.plugins.git.*

def call() {   // define the main function to be called by Jenkins pipeline scripting language (Groovy DSL). 
    def env = [:]     // Define a map for environment variables that will pass through all nodes in your build/deploy chain, e.g., 'env' is used here as an example variable name and value pair to be passed from Jenkins job configuration into the pipeline scripting language (Groovy DSL).
    env['BUILD_NUMBER'] = BUILD_NUMBER     // This will pass through all nodes in your build/deploy chain. 
        .replaceAll('[^a-zA-Z0-9]', '')   // Remove any non alphanumeric characters from the Jenkins Build Number to ensure uniqueness of each job run within a single pipeline instance (Jenkins Pipeline).   
    
def build_env = [:]  // Define another map for environment variables that will be used only in this specific stage/build.   e.g., 'build' is an example variable name and value pair to pass from Jenkin job configuration into the pipeline scripting language (Groovy DSL).
    build_env['ARTIFACTS'] = "http://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/artifactbucket/"  // This will only be used in this specific stage of your Jenkins Pipeline, and it'll pass through all nodes within the same pipeline instance (Jenkin).
    
    node('build') {   // Define a label for each job run that is unique to every build/deploy chain.  'node()' keyword specifies which executor will execute this block of code in Jenkins Pipeline scripting language DSL, here it’s set as the name ('Build').
        stage('Download Artifact') {   // Define a label for each job run that is unique to every build/deploy chain.  'stage()' keyword specifies which executor will execute this block of code in Jenkins Pipeline scripting language DSL, here it’s set as the name ('Stage').
            withCredentials([[$class: 'UsernamePasswordMultiBinding', credentialsID: '', usernameVariable: 'USERNAME_KEY', passwordVariable: 'PASSWORD_KEY']]) {  // Define a label for each job run that is unique to every build/deploy chain.   Here it’s set as the name ('Credentials').
                env.ARTIFACTS = "http://artifactbucket/" + BUILD_NUMBER    // This will only be used in this specific stage of your Jenkins Pipeline, and is passed through all nodes within same pipeline instance (Jenkin).  It's appending the build number to external URL.
                echo env   // Print out environment variables for debugging purposes - it’ll pass through each node inside a single job run in this case ('Build').    e.g., 'env'.println(“${USERNAME_KEY}”) – will print username from Jenkins credentials store (Jenkin).
                // Call the script to download artifacts using Git plugin and then deploy them on production servers, here it’ll pass through all nodes within same pipeline instance.   e.g., 'git' is used as an example of calling a Groovy DSL method from Jenkins Pipeline (Groovy).
                // Here you can use the git-remote Git plugin to download artifacts and then deploy them on production servers, here it’ll pass through all nodes within same pipeline instance.   e.g., 'git' is used as an example of calling a Groovy DSL method from Jenkins Pipeline (Groovy).
            }  // End withCredentials block - Define credentials for the build/deploy chain here it’s set to pass through all nodes within same pipeline instance.   e.g., 'credential'.println(“${USERNAME_KEY}”)    -- will print username from Jenkins credentia store (Jenkin).
        }  // End stage block - Define a label for each job run that is unique to every build/deploy chain here it’s set as the name ('Stage').   e.g., 'stage'.println(“${BUILD_NUMBER}”)    -- will print Jenkins Build Number (Jenkin).
        // End node block - Define a label for each job run that is unique to every build/deploy chain here it’s set as the name ('Build').   e.g., 'build'.println(“${BUILD
